opp.name: Skjodt-Barrett Foods_S2C+Spend_June26
opp.next_step: Mike Send follow up email with materials on contract management, spend analysis, supplier management, and sourcing events. Share collateral through a Digital Sales Room if needed. Offer support in building a business case for budget planning. Abeerah Follow up on next steps.
opp.ais_why:
• At Skjodt-Barrett the 'Merlin Agentic Platform was discussed,' with 'interest from MD in AI capabilities and supplier portal features' and highlighted 'autonomous negotiation agent and proactive risk management.'
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes and the MD engaged directly on AI capabilities, a senior-level positive signal.
• Evaluation is 6-8 months out with questions on Microsoft D365 integration, so the strong AI interest is paired with a longer timeline.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, MD Saidur Khan: 'we need better visibility into spend and supplier performance, and we want to automate contract workflows'

metrics.narrative: Khan articulated qualitative pain: manual procurement workflows, Excel-based spend analysis, and vendor compliance gaps driving inefficiency. He stated 'we need better visibility into spend and supplier performance, and we want to automate contract workflows'. No quantified impact provided (no cost, cycle time, headcount, error rate, or ROI target). Khan indicated the business case must be built 'to justify budget', implying internal stakeholders expect quantification but it has not yet been co-developed. Zycus offered to support building the business case with FMCG benchmarking, but this has not yet occurred. The gap is actionable: a quantified value case anchored to Khan's pain (e.g. X% reduction in maverick spend, Y days faster contract cycle, Z FTE savings in procurement ops) is required to anchor EB approval within the next 30-60 days. Without it, budget approval will stall.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: MD Saidur Khan (Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator)
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22: Khan articulated pain, scope, and interest in Merlin AI
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Khan: escalation to 'head of supply chain' required for budget approval, but no introduction offered
Next_Step__c 2026-07-01: Zycus committed to send collateral and business-case support; no buyer response in 9 days
champion.narrative: MD Saidur Khan (Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator) is the only buyer-side contact engaged to date. He initiated the discovery call, articulated pain and scope, and expressed interest in Merlin AI, which are positive indicators of intent. However, Khan has not yet demonstrated champion-level advocacy: (1) he has not introduced Zycus to the head of supply chain (the EB), despite stating that executive's approval is required, (2) he has not shared internal timelines, competitive context, or decision-maker names, and (3) he has not responded to Zycus's follow-up commitments (collateral and business-case support) in the 9 days since the discovery call. Khan's current engagement is passive evaluator, not active champion. His role (Strategic Sourcing Manager) suggests he has procurement domain expertise and likely influence with the head of supply chain, but that influence has not yet been activated on Zycus's behalf. The champion strength is weak-to-developing: Khan is engaged but not advocating. The immediate move is to re-engage Khan within 7 days to (a) deliver the committed collateral and business-case support, and (b) request an introduction to the head of supply chain, positioning it as 'confirming strategic alignment before we invest in a detailed ROI model'. If Khan cannot or will not facilitate that introduction within 30 days, he is not a champion and Zycus must find another path to the EB.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22: no competitor mentioned by Khan
Salesforce Competitors__c and related competitive fields: null or not populated
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce. Khan did not mention any incumbent vendor, alternative platform, or evaluation of other solutions during the discovery call. This is unusual for a deal of this scope and size in a mid-market FMCG company; it is unlikely Skjodt-Barrett is running a sole-sourced procurement without benchmarking alternatives. Three scenarios are possible: (1) Khan has not yet disclosed the competitive set (early-stage confidentiality), (2) the buyer is comparing Zycus against an internal build-vs-buy decision or status quo (Excel + email), or (3) Zycus is competing against an unknown incumbent or alternative platform (e.g. Microsoft D365 procurement extensions, Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP) that has not surfaced. The gap is a visibility risk: Zycus may be mis-positioning messaging, pricing, or differentiation without knowing the real comparison anchor. The immediate move is to ask Khan directly in the next call: 'As you build the business case, are you evaluating other platforms or vendors alongside Zycus, or is this a sole-source discussion?' and 'What are you using today for procurement and spend management?' to surface the status quo or incumbent. If Khan deflects, triangulate by asking whether the head of supply chain has vendor preferences or existing relationships.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, MD Saidur Khan: 'manual procurement workflows', 'Excel-based spend analysis', 'we need better visibility into spend and supplier performance'
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Khan: 'head of supply chain' must approve budget (implies senior pain owner)
identify_pain.narrative: Khan articulated three specific operational pains: (1) manual procurement workflows (no system automation, heavy email and spreadsheet reliance), (2) Excel-based spend analysis ('we need better visibility into spend and supplier performance'), and (3) vendor compliance gaps (implied by his mention of 'supplier management' as a priority area). He stated these pains are driving inefficiency and limiting procurement's strategic impact. The pain owner is Khan himself (Strategic Sourcing Manager), who is likely responsible for procurement operations and vendor oversight. However, Khan also indicated that the 'head of supply chain' (unmapped) must approve budget, implying that executive is the senior pain owner and strategic sponsor. The confirmed pain is clear and directly tied to the solution scope (S2C, Spend, CLM, Supplier Management). The gap is that the pain is not yet quantified (no cost, cycle time, or ROI impact), and the senior pain owner (head of supply chain) has not articulated it directly. The next move is to validate and quantify the pain with the head of supply chain in an executive alignment call within 30 days.

sources: No Avoma discussion of MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or SI involvement
Salesforce: no Current_Contract_Expiration__c or Paper Process fields populated
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and SI/partner involvement have not been discussed. The deal is in Qualified stage and 359 days from close; paper process clarity is not expected until Vendor Selected or later (typically 6-9 months into the cycle). However, two early signals are worth noting: (1) Khan is a Strategic Sourcing Manager, implying the buyer has a formal procurement function that may impose process rigor (RFP, vendor onboarding, standard terms), and (2) the account is a mid-market FMCG company, which may require InfoSec review for SaaS and integration scoping for ERP connectivity (spend data feeds). No contract expiration forcing function is present (Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null or not populated). The gap is appropriate for the stage; visibility will come when commercials begin (Shortlisted or later). The move is to ask Khan in the next call whether Skjodt-Barrett has standard procurement/legal processes Zycus should plan for, and whether an SI or implementation partner will be involved.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, MD Saidur Khan: escalation to 'head of supply chain' is required for budget approval
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: only Khan (Evaluator) mapped, no EB or Executive Sponsor contact
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. Khan (Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator) explicitly stated that escalation to the 'head of supply chain' is required for budget approval, naming that role as the budget authority. However, the head of supply chain has not been identified by name, has no Salesforce contact role, and has not appeared on any call. No commercial or pricing discussion has occurred (the discovery call was scope and pain only). Khan's role is evaluator and influencer, not budget holder. The evidence shows budget authority sits one level up in the org chart, but that person is not yet in the deal. This is a critical gap: without EB engagement within 30 days, the business case and commercials will have no anchor, and budget approval will stall. The immediate move is to ask Khan to introduce the head of supply chain for a brief executive alignment call within the next 14 days, positioning it as 'confirming strategic fit before we invest in a detailed ROI model'.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22: only Khan present, no senior execs
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Khan: escalation to 'head of supply chain' required for budget approval
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Khan: 'the budget cycle is starting now'
decision_process.narrative: The call sequence to date is minimal: one discovery call with Khan (Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator) on 2026-06-22. No senior executives, no procurement director, no EB have joined. Khan stated escalation to the head of supply chain is required for budget approval, implying a two-tier process: (1) Khan evaluates and recommends, (2) head of supply chain approves budget and strategic fit. The approval chain is unmapped beyond that. Khan mentioned 'the budget cycle is starting now', suggesting timing aligns with fiscal planning (likely calendar-year 2027 budget lock). However, no formal process milestones (RFI due date, vendor selection date, contract signature target) have been shared. The process the buyer is running is still forming; Khan is in discovery and has not yet escalated internally. The deal is in the right stage (Qualified) for this level of process clarity, but progression to the relevant stakeholder requires (a) Khan escalating to the head of supply chain within 30 days, and (b) Zycus securing a direct call with that executive to confirm strategic fit and timeline.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, MD Saidur Khan: scope articulated as S2C, Spend, CLM, Sourcing, Merlin
Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Khan: 'we want to see how your AI works in procurement'
Products__c: iContract;iSource;iSupplier;Merlin (confirms scope alignment)
decision_criteria.narrative: Khan outlined scope (S2C, Spend Analytics, CLM, Sourcing, AI/Merlin) and pain points (manual workflows, Excel spend analysis, vendor compliance gaps), which imply evaluation criteria around automation depth, spend visibility, contract lifecycle coverage, and AI capability. He stated 'we want to see how your AI works in procurement' and confirmed interest in Merlin for sourcing and spend intelligence. However, no formal RFP, RFI, or weighted scorecard has been shared. No competitive benchmark or test-case validation discussed. The criteria are inferred from pain and scope, not confirmed through a structured evaluation process. For a 100K deal with a 12-month cycle, the buyer will likely run an RFI or vendor comparison in the Shortlisted stage; criteria will crystallize then. The current partial read is sufficient for Qualified stage, but formalizing criteria (via a BRD review or use-case workshop) is the next gate to the relevant stakeholder.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS fields are null or not populated for this deal. Based on the Avoma discovery call, Khan expressed clear interest in AI: 'we want to see how your AI works in procurement'. Merlin is in the product scope. This signals AI Curious tier, potentially AI Hungry if the business case can be anchored on AI-driven procurement value (sourcing intelligence, contract risk detection, spend optimization). The AI positioning is under-activated: no Merlin demo, no agentic narrative, no AI-driven ROI case delivered yet. The next move is to make Merlin the centerpiece of the business-case collateral and a follow-on demo, positioning it as the differentiator that drives quantified value (e.g. faster sourcing, better contract compliance, proactive spend insights).

cro_panel.header: Skjodt-Barrett Foods Inc — Qualified · $100K · closes 25 Jun

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by momentum 56 vs stage-expected 50; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 56 (moving forward): Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 42 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 42 and momentum 56, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls).

confidence_signals.summary: Confidence signals are mixed. Positive: Khan articulated clear pain and scope, expressed interest in Merlin AI, and the product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin) is well-aligned. Negative: single-thread exposure (only Khan engaged, 0 EB/DM contact), no quantified business case, no competitive visibility, and Khan has not responded to follow-up commitments in 9 days post-discovery. The deal is live per pulse (rep outreach active), but buyer engagement is thin and has not yet deepened. No cooling or ghost risk at this stage, but if Khan does not re-engage within 7 days or EB mapping does not progress within 30 days, confidence will degrade rapidly.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-22, qualified same day, 9 days in Qualified stage. Close date 2027-06-25 (359 days out) allows ample runway for a 12-15 month enterprise motion. No forward slip detected. Pace is not the issue; the stage is appropriate for the timeline. However, the deal carries two stacked contributors: (1) single-threaded (only MD Saidur Khan engaged, 0 EB/DM contact), and (2) low engagement depth (one discovery call, no follow-on buyer response to collateral commitments made 9 days ago). Per verdict guide rails, 1 contributor forces At Risk; 2+ would force Off Track, but engagement is borderline (rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply per pulse). Defaulting to At Risk with close monitoring: if Khan does not re-engage within 7 days or EB mapping does not progress within 30 days, this downgrades to Off Track.
evidence: Single contact role: MD Saidur Khan (Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion mapped. Khan stated escalation to 'head of supply chain' is required for budget approval, but that executive is unmapped and unengaged. (Source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22, Salesforce OpportunityContactRole)

One buyer call (discovery, 2026-06-22). Khan articulated pain (manual procurement, Excel spend analysis, vendor compliance gaps) and scope (S2C + Spend + CLM + Sourcing + Merlin AI), but no quantified business case, no competitive mention, no timeline beyond 'budget cycle starting now'. (Source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-22)

Zycus committed to send collateral and budget-case support on 2026-06-22; Next Step logged same day. No buyer reply or follow-on activity in 9 days. Rep-side outreach assumed live per pulse, but buyer silence is a yellow flag. (Source: Next_Step__c 2026-07-01, LastActivityDate 2026-06-22)

No field history changes post-creation (stage, amount, close date static). No tasks, no additional events. Thin evidence base for a deal with 15+ procurement stakeholders on the account. (Source: OpportunityFieldHistory, Task/Event queries)
north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal (Qualified, 359 days to close) with fresh activity (last touch 9 days ago) but stalled by single-thread exposure, unmapped economic buyer, and no quantified business case to anchor budget approval for a 12+ month cycle.

flags: Multi-thread urgently: Only 1 buyer-side contact role (MD Saidur Khan, Evaluator). The account has 15+ procurement and supply chain contacts including Brian Reesor (Supply Chain Officer), Isfahan Ali Bodhani (Director of Procurement), and Nirantha Wijemanne (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager) who are not yet engaged. Reach the economic buyer (likely head of supply chain per Khan's escalation path) and map the full buying committee within the next 14 days to avoid single-thread risk.

Co-develop the quantified business case: Buyer articulated qualitative pain (manual PO-to-contract compliance) but no quantified savings, FTE impact, or cycle-time reduction. Partner with MD Saidur Khan and his supervisor to build a data-driven ROI model anchored to the relevant stakeholder' sourcing event volume, contract compliance error rate, and tail-spend negotiation efficiency. This is the forcing function to escalate to the head of supply chain and secure budget.

Lead with Merlin agentic AI as the wedge: Buyer showed strong engagement on agentic AI for RFQ generation and autonomous negotiation on the discovery call 22 Jun 2026. Position Merlin as the Phase 1 centerpiece for tail-spend automation and RFQ efficiency, differentiating Zycus from legacy procurement platforms. Make this the demo anchor and tie it to the quantified business case (e.g., 'Merlin can reduce tail-spend negotiation cycle time by 60% and auto-generate RFQs for 80% of low-value events').
Single-thread risk: Only 1 contact (Khan, Evaluator) mapped. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion identified. Multi-thread within 14 days.
Economic Buyer unmapped: Khan indicated budget approval requires escalation to head of supply chain, but that executive is unidentified and unengaged. Map and engage within 14 days.
No quantified value case: Business problem and ROI not articulated by buyer. Co-build business case with Khan within 14 days, then validate with EB.
Competition unknown: No competitor named. Ask Khan what they use today and whether evaluating other vendors, within 7 days.
Champion weak: Khan is interested (requested collateral, wants business-case help) but has not facilitated EB introduction or shown internal advocacy. Assess champion potential within 14 days.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is early-stage (Qualified, 9 days old) with fresh activity, but carries three enterprise motion gaps that block progression: (1) single-thread exposure (only Khan engaged, 0 EB/DM contact), (2) Economic Buyer unmapped despite Khan naming the role, and (3) no quantified business case to anchor budget approval. Immediate priorities: re-engage Khan within 7 days, secure EB introduction within 30 days, co-build quantified ROI case within 60 days.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is unknown. No competitor named by buyer or logged in Salesforce. Khan did not mention any incumbent, alternative vendor, or evaluation process during the discovery call. This is a visibility gap: Zycus may be competing against an undisclosed incumbent (e.g. Microsoft D365, Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, or a legacy ERP procurement module), status quo (Excel + email), or an internal build-vs-buy decision. The immediate move is to surface the competitive context by asking Khan directly: 'What are you using today for procurement and spend management?' and 'Are you evaluating other platforms alongside Zycus?' Triangulate with the head of supply chain when engaged.

items.quote: Khan requested materials to support internal evaluation and business-case building
items.requirement: Provide collateral on contract management, spend analysis, supplier management, and sourcing events.

items.quote: Khan stated 'we need to build a business case to justify budget' and Zycus offered to support with FMCG benchmarking examples
items.requirement: Business case / budget justification with FMCG benchmarking for internal approval

ai_positioning_strength.score: AI Curious (inferred from buyer interest, no AIS score available)
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI appetite signal from Khan: 'we want to see how your AI works in procurement'. Merlin is in the product scope (Products__c: Merlin) and Khan's interest is confirmed. However, no deeper AI narrative (agentic procurement, I2O, negotiation intelligence) has been delivered yet. The discovery call was scope and pain only; Merlin positioning has not yet been activated. AIS fields are null (no AIS scoring in this org or not yet populated for this deal), so the read is based solely on the call. Khan's interest suggests AI Curious tier, potentially AI Hungry if the business case anchors on AI-driven value (e.g. Merlin-powered sourcing intelligence, contract risk detection). The next move is to deliver a Merlin-focused narrative as part of the business-case collateral and demo, positioning AI as the differentiator vs status quo and any incumbent competitor.

items.quote: Khan articulated need for 'automated contract workflows' and 'supplier management'
Khan stated 'we need better visibility into spend and supplier performance' and mentioned Excel-based analysis as a pain point
Khan: 'we want to see how your AI works in procurement'
Khan included 'sourcing events' in scope discussion; Zycus committed to share collateral on this

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong scope alignment: Khan's stated priorities (S2C, Spend Analytics, CLM, Sourcing, AI/Merlin) map directly to the Products logged (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin). Khan expressed interest in 'seeing how your AI works in procurement', which aligns with Zycus's Merlin positioning. No misalignment or gaps surfaced in the discovery call. However, the fit is based on a single evaluator's articulation of need; it has not yet been validated with the Economic Buyer (head of supply chain) or through a structured BRD/RFP process. The next gate is to confirm strategic fit with the EB and validate use-case requirements through a deeper demo or workshop.

hard.ais_why:
• At Skjodt-Barrett the 'Merlin Agentic Platform was discussed,' with 'interest from MD in AI capabilities and supplier portal features' and highlighted 'autonomous negotiation agent and proactive risk management.'
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes and the MD engaged directly on AI capabilities, a senior-level positive signal.
• Evaluation is 6-8 months out with questions on Microsoft D365 integration, so the strong AI interest is paired with a longer timeline.
hard.next_step:
Mike

  • Send follow up email with materials on contract management, spend analysis, supplier management, and sourcing events.
  • Share collateral through a Digital Sales Room if needed.
  • Offer support in building a business case for budget planning.

Abeerah 

  • Follow up on next steps.
